title: WHATWG-Informed HTML Trees, Cheerio, parse5, and Parser Differentials · Compiler Case: Parser Differential

Predict authorization, URL identity, runtime admission, Promise ownership, resource bounds, accessibility impact, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. steps.title: Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Predict Parser Differential · Theory Parser Differential
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript team handles case 9333 during an isolated browser, package, and restore smoke. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove Cheerio loading modes, malformed tree repair, parser differentials, budgets, and source-tree distinctions; every Promise, stream, listener, timer, socket, file, browser context, and report sink needs one cleanup owner; then change one duplicate, accessibility state, axe result, resource budget, browser readiness, or manual-review condition without overclaiming. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
